# Transcrição: 14-High Speed Chase virtual

- **Fonte:** 14-High Speed Chase virtual
- **Duração:** 06:24
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Linda Diaz steps out of her house and she looks more beautiful than she has ever looked before, which is kind of ridiculous because she looks exactly like she has looked before. She's wearing exactly the same thing I have seen her every single day of my life. It is an orange and white McDonald's uniform with a red hat and black shoes. It is exactly the thing I have seen her for my entire life, but somehow it's different this time. I think it's because she's not framed by the friar later. She's sort of like framed by a house. She's outside of work and this is the first time I have ever seen her outside the restaurant. And I am in love with Linda Diaz. In truth, I'm 16 years old, so I'm in love with like 12 girls. I'm basically in love with any girl who would choose to like me, which has not happened yet, so I'm keeping my options open, but she is definitely in my top 12. Linda and I work at McDonald's together. We work in the drive-thru in McDonald's parlance. That tells you that we are excellent at our job. We work in the hardest little cube in the place and we sort of have this ballet that we've developed over the last year where we can pass Big Macs and money and barbecue sauce. It's like almost sensual the way we move, except I don't think Linda thinks it's sensual, which is part of the problem. I can't get her attention because as a 16-year-old boy, I understand that the way you get girls to like you is you show disinterest in learning, you disregard and disrespect teachers, and you punch people whenever you can. Like apathy and aggression are what girls are really into. And the problem is at school I can do all those things. It hasn't actually worked yet, but it's like a slow roll. Eventually figure it out. But school can't fire me. I can do all these terrible things at school and yet I get to come back every day and continue to do them. But at McDonald's, if I do any of them, I'll be fired. So I have to be like on my best behavior at McDonald's so Linda has not noticed my extraordinary qualities yet. But this is different. I'm picking her up and bringing her to school. I got the car ready. I cleaned it. I washed it out. I got a tears for fears cassette for her. It's a bullshit band. I don't even know what tears for fears means. Like you're supposed to cry when you're afraid. But I got it set up and playing because I know she loves the band. And so she gets in the car and she's happy and I'm happy. It's a 15 minute drive to McDonald's. It's the same ride I do every single day. And so we take off and I'm just thinking how am I going to press this girl. And it's the same ride I do every day, which is why I should have known that there was going to be the cop hiding in the trees who has already pulled me over twice in the last six months for speeding. And as I go by, I see him. I look at my speedometer and I say, damn it. And I can see him already pulling out. But because I've done this ride so many times and I think because Linda's in my car and because I know that there's a hard left coming up on the road, I think I might be able to

## [03:03 - 06:04]

get away from this cop. It's a hard left and then it's a hard right and then there's a street full of streets. And this might be the thing I need to do to get Linda to like me. So I punch it and I go around the hard right and I look in the rear view mirror and I don't see the cop. So presumably he can't see me. And then I make the hard left and I look in the rear view mirror and still I don't think he can see me. And then I make my call. I take the left. It's not the left of a street though. It's a driveway. It's a driveway that runs up to the side of a house and there's no cars in it. So turn and I go up the driveway past the house and I pull into the backyard of the house behind the house. It is amazing. It is a nine second high speed chase and I think I have done it and I looked to Linda to think my God, did you see that? And she says, what the hell is wrong with you? And I realized she didn't know we were in a high speed chase because she didn't see the cop and it was only nine seconds long. So I explained to her that we were just in a high speed chase and I think we did it and she says, what the hell is wrong with you? And she says there could have been kids in the backyard. There could be a person in the house. There could have been a dog. The cop could get to us. What are you doing? And I'm thinking apathy, aggression, isn't this what you want? And she tells me all the reasons I'm stupid and all the reasons why I make bad decisions and she makes me feel really bad about myself. And she says, bring me to the restaurant and I tell her, I can't. We kind of have to wait here a little bit because the cop might still be looking for me. So we need to wait like 15 minutes. So I sit in the most silent 15 minutes of my life in that car with Linda Diaz. And when we finally get to McDonald's, the two stars of the restaurant are 15 minutes late for the first times in our life and there's two other people in the drive through. So we have to do registers that day, which is like the land of stupidity. It's like the dumbest people they put on registers and we are stuck there all day and Linda doesn't speak to me for the rest of the day and for two weeks she will not speak to me except to ask me for a Big Mac or barbecue sauce. I like to think that that is the day I learned that girls are not interested in apathy and aggression. That they would prefer to have guys in their lives who are sort of respectful and trustworthy. Guys who would presumably take care of them in ways that are not dangerous and stupid. It turns out that girls don't actually want guys who punch other people and drive their cars behind houses. They just want decent human beings who put thought into them instead of into myself. I would like to tell you that was the day I learned that lesson but it was not. It was the first step on a journey and when I finally got my first girlfriend when I was

## [06:04 - 06:24]

17, her name was Laura, the first love of my life. I like to think I was a little bit better of a boyfriend because of what Linda told me that day behind that house. And that maybe even through all of those times I became slightly better every day. Thank you.
